objective: Test whether the Wave 58 topology feature-ablation no-go is sensitive to small deterministic logistic-probe hyperparameter choices.
non_negotiables: A topology result is claim-eligible only if the same setting beats train-majority on both dev and test.
The sweep must stay on the Wave 57 topology-balanced split.
Test-only improvements must remain no-go evidence.
This is a stability diagnostic, not a new model class.

tasks.prompt: Add and run a topology-balanced feature-group hyperparameter sweep over the Wave 55 rpf36 exact shard.
acceptance: Each feature group has 18 deterministic hyperparameter trials.
The claim candidate count is recorded.
Any dev/test majority-beating result is explicitly listed.

tasks.prompt: Record Wave 61 as a hyperparameter-stability no-go for topology feature-group probes.
commands: python3 -m py_compile engine/ml_model.py engine/test.py agents/network.py agents/label_schema.py
acceptance: Docs state that the best dev-only result and best test-only result do not align.
Docs route next work toward genuinely new value supply or a materially different model class.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_61_topology_ablation_hyperparameter_sweep.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The Wave 61 plan is internally consistent.
The Wave 61 sweep report is valid JSON.
